```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width < PAD then PAD else x.width)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
    c.kind == x.kind &&
    c.value == x.value &&
    c.width >= PAD &&
    c.width >= NumDigits(c.value) &&
    (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
    (x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD) &&
    Wellformed(c)
{ }
```

The implementation is straightforward: `Canonicalize` preserves the kind and value, and sets the width to the maximum of the current width and the minimum padding constant `PAD` (which is 4).

The specification captures:
- **kind and value preservation**: The kind and value never change
- **minimum width**: The canonical width is at least `PAD`
- **wellformedness preserved**: The result is wellformed (width can hold the value)
- **no-op on already-canonical ids**: If the input width is already ≥ 4, the width doesn't change
- **canonicalization of narrow ids**: If the input width is < 4, it becomes exactly 4
